programmes.show_description: 1994. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. Jean Paul interviews John Waters, Victoria Abril, and Germany's biggest pop phenomenon, the Irish-American Kelly Family.
1964. Director: Joseph W. Sarno. Stars Marla Ellis, Eve Harris & Sheila Britt. Three friends want to become stars, they ensure endless frauds and casting couches which put a strain on their bond.
1998. Documentary. Directed by Ted Newson. King of Horror, Christopher Lee, takes us on a frightening journey from the earliest horror movies to modern day slashers!
1968. A brilliant look at the work of the Press Association with its teleprinters and fast movement to get the news out.
Theft of Excalibur. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. A knight is held to ransom. The price for his release is King Arthur's sword, Excalibur.
A Christmas to Remember. 1964. Stars Don Mason, David Graham & Ray Barrett. When Troy meets the orphaned son of an ex-WASP aquanaut, he decides to give the boy a Christmas to remember.
1965. Matter of Wife and Death. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. Someone tries to sink a ship carrying Honey and a woman who hired her.
1960. Adventure. Directed by Charles F. Haas & Sandy Howard. Stars Gordon Scott, Eve Brent & Rickie Sorensen. Tarzan must fight off the vengeful brother of a ruthless hunter he defeated.
1966. Family. Starring Brian Blessed, William Burleigh, Anthony Honour & Kate Nicholls. Gary determines to get a tree for children who will be in hospital for Christmas. Directed by Jim Clark.
1959. Sci-fi. Director: Arthur Crabtree. Stars Marshall Thompson & Terry Kilburn. A captain is assigned to investigate disappearances that residents are blaming on nuclear experiments.
Son of Lafe Returns. 1964. Stars Buddy Ebsen, Conlan Carter & Donna Douglas. Lafe Crick brings his son, Dub, to Beverly Hills to try some match making between him and Elly, but Jed is suspicious.
1943. Western. Directed by George Archainbaud. Stars Richard Dix, Jane Wyatt & Albert Dekker. A cowboy's life takes a turn after he stops a gang of thieves from robbing a bank in the town.
Caught in the Spiders Den. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Black widow manages to take over the duo's brains again and continues her crime spree using the perfect disguise!
2000. Drama. Directed by Catherine Morshead. Stars Jenny Agutter & Richard Attenborough. Three children are forced to move to Yorkshire with their mother after their father is falsely imprisoned.
1961. The Silent Weapon. Directed by Peter Duffell. Staring Geoffrey Keen, Stanley Morgan and Norma Parnell. A horse rider is found dead in suspicious circumstances. (S1, E35)
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Bobby Crush, Jenny Hanley, Mike Read, Joan Sims, Bernie Winters & Lena Zavaroni.
The King of Knaves Affair. 1964. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Paul Stevens. A Middle Eastern leader is plotting to ruin the economy. Solo & Kuryakin adopt aliases to foil the plan.
We ride back to Devon in 1955; a reel takes us to Sunderland in the 70s with Sir Tommy Steele! There's footage of British bandleader Henry Hall and we talk Titch & Quackers.
Coup de Grace. 1989. Stars Robert Powell, Anthony Valentine & Joanna David. Hannay becomes ensnared by a web of lies when he meets the beautiful Sybil Verney at a race meeting.
Only When it Hurts. 1983. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Ida breaks her tooth on Mrs Price's sausage rolls. How will she get on at her first dentist trip in 28 years?
The Dead Man's Evidence. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, John Stone & Brian Worth. Blackmail and greed turn grave when Dick notices a secret affair. Things all change when a fight ends in manslaughter.
The Case of the Penny Plains. 1964. Stars John Barrie, William Gaunt & Charles Morgan. An attempt is made on a wealthy woman's life. Marriott is put on the case but finds himself falling for her.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Paul Melba, Janet Brown and Dawson Chance and play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1977. Comedy. Director: Stanley Long. Stars Chris Neil, Suzy Kendall, Harry H. Corbett. While his boss is away, Bob helps a model being blackmailed, finds a dead body & ends up in a cabaret club.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
1971. Comedy. Directed by Bob Kellett. Stars Frankie Howerd, Patrick Cargill & Michael Hordern. A Roman slave inadvertently possesses a scroll which details a plot against the Emperor Nero.
1963. Who Killed Harris Crown? Directed by Don Weis. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. The murder of Mr. Crown is complicated by the fact that Mrs. Crown is pregnant - not by her husband.
1965. The Project Man. Gig Young, David Niven and Charles Boyer are The Rogues, members of two related families of international forgers and conmen.
The Youngest Outlaw. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Rufus Cruickshank. While hunting, Robin & Little John find a boy who has run away from Waldern Castle to join Robin's outlaws.
Dan Tempest and the Amazons. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Joan Sims & Neil Hallett. A French pirate arrives in New Providence and attempts to sell seven women.
The Cuckoo. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Gessler's problem is compounded further when he receives a message demanding taxes Gessler hasn't collected.
Lobo Lawman. Director: Aaron Spelling. A U.S. Marshal arrives in town with a bandit as prisoner. Ringo recognises him as the man who killed his uncle and challenges the lawman to a fight.
1962. Fantasy. Directed by Nathan Juran. Stars Kerwin Mathews, Judi Meredith, & Torin Thatcher. The treacherous Pendragon plots to gain the throne of Cornwall. Can anyone stop the evil sorcerer?
1956. Directed by Eric Marquis. From Kilkeel in Northern Ireland to Lincoln Cathedral, travel across the four corners of the United Kingdom in 1956.
1963. Crime. Directed by Peter Maxwell. Stars Anthony Steel, Zena Marshall & Conrad Phillips. A woman is abducted by a wristwatch smuggling gang who believe she knows too much.
1939. Horror. Directed by Walter Summers. Stars Bela Lugosi & Hugh Williams. A series of drownings in the Thames starts Inspector Holt on the trail of one of London's most sinister murderers.
Lady of the Orchids. 1954. Stars Lilli Palmer, John Howard & Robert Paige. Neglected by her husband, Stacy goes out with a wealthy bachelor but the evening ends in a fatal car crash.
The Lion and the Eagle. 1962. Stars Dermot Walsh, Trader Faulkner & Alan Haywood. Richard returns to London for his coronation, only to find that his brother, John, has kidnapped their mother.
1957. Comedy. Director: David Mcdonald. Stars Gordon Harker, Marie Lohr, John Lode & Billie Whitelaw. At a small hotel, Albert, an aged but shrewd waiter, is a past master at the art of fiddling.

programmes.show_name: Selsey Pavilion: The Journey So Far
programmes.show_description: 2025. Created by students of The Academy Selsey and Millstream Productions, this documentary explores the history of the Selsey Pavilion, a pre World War One Art Deco theatre & cinema.
1946. Drama. Director Lawrence Huntington. Robert Newton stars as Captain David Grant, a British agent who leads an attempt to save scientist Professor Hansen (Martin Miller) from the Nazis.
1960. Crime Drama. Director: Basil Dearden. Stars Jack Hawkins, Richard Attenborough, Nigel Patrick & Roger Livesey. Ex-army men are recruited for a bank robbery that requires military precision.
The Dead Man's Evidence. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, John Stone & Brian Worth. Blackmail and greed turn grave when Dick notices a secret affair. Things all change when a fight ends in manslaughter.
Happy St David's Day! Enjoy the brilliant Llantrisant Choir, then Mike & Noel have a great short film full of footballers in 1945 as well as a wonderful Scopitone of Gary Lewis & The Playboys!
1989. Stars Diana Rigg, James Wilby & David McCallum. Helena decides to avenge every betrayal. She leaves the ultimate betrayal till last...
1959. Action. Director: Terence Fisher. Stars Guy Rolfe, Allan Cuthbertson & Andrew Cruickshank. A captain lobbies to investigate the criminal Thugee Cult of Kali, an organised crime group.
A delightful and entertaining film about the on-going search to find a permanent home in Brighton for the mass of Max Miller memorabilia The Max Miller Appreciation Society holds.
Directed by Francis A Searle. A 1969 comedy short centring around workmen digging a hole. Starring Arthur Lowe, Victor Maddern, Bill Maynard and Tim Barrett.
